_meta.title: OctoPrint тАФ native state flags тЖТ normalized lifecycle
_meta.family: OctoPrint host controller (fronts a USB/serial printer)
_meta.confidence: ЁЯЯб source-read for the 9 documented flags; тЪк for the two source-only flags and the composite precedence

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from OctoPrint's official API docs + AGPL server source. No code copied. Validatable hardware-free via the official Docker image + bundled Virtual Printer. Target 1.11.x (floor 1.11.8).

notes: Parse the `state.flags` BOOLEANS, never the human `state.text`. The same object arrives in the REST poll (`GET /api/printer` тЖТ `state:{text,flags,error?}`) and the SockJS `current`/`history` pushes.
`Operational` is ambiguous: it is both 'idle/ready' AND the base state under which the printing/paused flags ride тАФ read `printing`/`paused`/`pausing`/`cancelling` BEFORE concluding standby.
The server emits 11 flags; only 9 are documented here тАФ `resuming` and `finishing` are source-only (тЪк, confirm on a live instance). Prefer them when present, else derive from the state string.
`GET /api/printer` returns `409` when the printer is not operational тАФ a valid 'not connected' signal, not a transport failure.
`sdReady` / `ready` / `closedOrError` are link/SD flags, not lifecycle-print states. There is no explicit 'complete' flag; completion is inferred from `finishing` + job progress.

mappings.native: operational (and not printing and not paused)
mappings.note: the ambiguous base 'Operational' тАФ read the print flags first
source-only flag; text `Resuming` is the fallback tell
source-only flag; a finishing hint, not a distinct 'complete' flag
